concerns.text: The inconsistency‑detection rule adds an extra tolerance clause – “or exceeds max(0.01, 0.2 × reconstructed p)” – which is not present in the original idea. The idea’s detection criteria are limited to a simple absolute p‑value difference > 0.05 (plus the other relative‑difference rules). Introducing this additional bound subtly redefines what counts as an “inconsistent” summary, thereby altering the measurement of the prevalence outcome.

FR-027 introduces a bias‑assessment requirement (source over‑representation, geographic concentration) that was not part of the original idea’s research question. Adding this functional requirement expands the project’s scope beyond auditing prevalence of statistical inconsistencies.

responses.response: Removed the extra tolerance clause from FR-004, retaining only the original absolute p‑value difference > 0.05 as the inconsistency criterion.
responses.what_changed: Edited FR-004 text to delete "or exceeds max(0.01, 0.2 × reconstructed p)".

new_concerns.text: Functional Requirement FR-028 (documentation & quickstart guide) is not referenced by any user story. Anchor it to a relevant story (e.g., US‑4) or remove it.
Success Criterion SC-028 (quickstart guide verification) is not linked to any user story. Add a traceability reference to a story or delete the criterion.

FR-027 introduces a bias‑assessment requirement (source over‑representation, geographic concentration) that was not part of the original idea’s research question. Adding this functional requirement expands the project’s scope beyond auditing prevalence of statistical inconsistencies.

concerns.text: The specification still does not provide an explicit a‑priori power/sample‑size justification for the binomial prevalence test. FR‑029 only states that the required sample size shall be computed and documented, but the plan lacks a clear description of how the baseline proportion (0.05), target power (0.80), and effect size (0.10) are used to determine the required number of URLs before data collection.

SC‑003 requires Monte‑Carlo validation of each statistical test implementation with a tolerance ≤ 0.01, yet the implementation plan does not describe any Monte‑Carlo cross‑check procedure, number of replicates, or how the tolerance will be verified.

FR-027 introduces a bias‑assessment requirement (source over‑representation, geographic concentration) that was not part of the original idea’s research question. Adding this functional requirement expands the project’s scope beyond auditing prevalence of statistical inconsistencies.

responses.response: Provided a concise justification for FR-027 (bias assessment) linking it to the core research question, thereby keeping the requirement within scope. No removal of the requirement was performed.

new_concerns.text: The specification still does not provide an explicit a‑priori power/sample‑size justification for the binomial prevalence test. FR‑029 only states that the required sample size shall be computed and documented, but the plan lacks a clear description of how the baseline proportion (0.05), target power (0.80), and effect size (0.10) are used to determine the required number of URLs before data collection.

SC‑003 requires Monte‑Carlo validation of each statistical test implementation with a tolerance ≤ 0.01, yet the implementation plan does not describe any Monte‑Carlo cross‑check procedure, number of replicates, or how the tolerance will be verified.

concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-027 uses a `[deferred]` placeholder for the domain‑over‑representation threshold, while Functional Requirement FR-027 explicitly defines the threshold as 30 % of the corpus. This mismatch leaves the success criterion undefined and contradicts the functional requirement.

responses.response: Removed the extra tolerance clause from FR-004, keeping only the absolute p‑value difference > 0.05 as the detection rule, matching the original idea.
responses.what_changed: Edited FR-004 to delete "or exceeds max(0.01, 0.2 × reconstructed p)" clause.

new_concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-027 uses a `[deferred]` placeholder for the domain‑over‑representation threshold, while Functional Requirement FR-027 explicitly defines the threshold as 30 % of the corpus. This mismatch leaves the success criterion undefined and contradicts the functional requirement.
